tasks.title: Initialize Astro project
tasks.description: Scaffold the Astro app on top of the existing package.json (astro dep + scripts already present). pnpm only.
steps: Scaffold src structure with the empty Astro template (pnpm, not npm) without clobbering the existing package.json/lockfile
Set pnpm-workspace.yaml allowBuilds: sharp: true (required by astro:assets) and esbuild: true
Verify the dev server runs with `pnpm start`

tasks.title: Set up project folder structure
tasks.description: Establish the directory layout. Source images live in src/ so astro:assets can optimize them; public/ holds only static-path assets.
steps: Create src/pages, src/layouts, src/components, src/content, src/styles
Create src/assets/paintings and src/assets/ceramics for optimizable source images
Reserve public/ for favicon, OG image, and anything that must keep an exact path
Add .gitignore entries for node_modules, dist, .env

tasks.title: Configure Astro
tasks.description: Set up astro.config.mjs. Image optimization is built into astro:assets (no integration to add).
steps: Set output to 'static'
Set the production site URL (required by sitemap and canonical URLs)
Add the @astrojs/sitemap integration
Add a robots.txt (in public/) pointing to sitemap.xml
Confirm astro:assets image optimization works (sharp available)

tasks.title: Build global styles and design tokens
tasks.description: One global stylesheet of CSS custom properties, imported in BaseLayout. Almost no hardcoded values anywhere.
steps: Create src/styles/tokens.css with custom properties: colors, type scale, spacing, breakpoints, --font-display, --font-body
Self-host fonts: add .woff2 files, @font-face with font-display: swap, preload the display face
Use scoped <style> per component; reference tokens, do not hardcode values

tasks.title: Set up content collections (Content Layer API)
tasks.description: Model paintings and ceramics as Astro content collections (Astro 6 Content Layer API) so pages map over them at build time.
steps: Create src/content.config.ts (root of src/, not inside src/content)
Define a glob loader per collection over data/markdown entries
Schema per Artwork: title, year, medium, images: z.array(z.object({ src: image(), alt: z.string() })).min(1).max(5), video: z.string().url().optional()
First image in the array is the Cover; src images resolve to src/assets/paintings or src/assets/ceramics

tasks.title: Seed placeholder content
tasks.description: All-placeholder seed data that exercises every gallery and detail-page code path. Real content swaps in before launch.
steps: Download royalty-free placeholder images in varied orientations (portrait + landscape) into src/assets/paintings and src/assets/ceramics
Ceramic A: multiple images + a real public YouTube URL -> full detail page (images + embed)
Ceramic B: multiple images, no video -> detail page, images only
Ceramic C: single image -> no detail page, non-clickable Cover
Painting A: single image, no video -> non-clickable Cover (the common case)
Painting B: multiple images -> detail page (proves a painting can get one)
Lorem-ish title/year/medium; alt text on every image

tasks.title: Configure Netlify deployment
tasks.description: Wire the repo to Netlify so every push to main deploys automatically (see ADR 0001).
steps: Add netlify.toml with build command (pnpm build) and publish directory (dist)
Connect the GitHub repo to a new Netlify site
Confirm a deploy succeeds on the empty project

tasks.title: Build base layout
tasks.description: Shared HTML shell for all pages: head, meta, global token stylesheet, and a content slot.
steps: Create src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro with <html>, <head>, <body>
Accept title and description props; render <title>, meta description, and a canonical URL
Add Open Graph + twitter:card=summary_large_image tags; ogImage prop defaults to the site default OG image (TODO: point at an image in src/assets)
Add charset, viewport, and a favicon
Import the global tokens stylesheet
Include the Header and a <slot /> for page content

tasks.title: Build site header and nav
tasks.description: Header with artist name/title top-left and a hamburger top-right (all viewports) that drops down the nav.
steps: Create src/components/Header.astro
Add name ('Josephine Florence') and subtitle ('Abstract Painter / Ceramicist') top-left in the display font
Add hamburger top-right on all viewports with links: About, Painting Gallery, Ceramics Gallery, Contact (no Shop)
Wire up open/close toggle (minimal <script> / small island)

tasks.title: Build Home page
tasks.description: Landing page with name/title and a full-bleed two-image hero diptych.
steps: Create src/pages/index.astro using BaseLayout
Two edge-to-edge images as a 50/50 diptych; object-fit: cover on a shared aspect ratio
Stack vertically (each full-width) on mobile via the breakpoint token
Hero images: loading=eager, fetchpriority=high, preloaded (they are the LCP)

tasks.title: Build About page
tasks.description: Bio with a size-capped photo floated left and text wrapping around it. Not full-bleed (image quality uncertain).
steps: Create src/pages/about.astro using BaseLayout
Photo floated left (float: left), max-width: min(40%, 360px), never upscaled beyond source; bio text wraps around it with a margin
Mobile: drop the float, photo on top, centered, keep the max-width (never blow up a low-res image); lazy-loaded

tasks.title: Build Painting Gallery page
tasks.description: Paintings from the collection in a two-column grid, scrolling vertically.
steps: Create src/pages/paintings.astro using BaseLayout
Query the paintings collection with getCollection()
Render one Cover (images[0]) per Artwork, two per row, with <Image> (AVIF+WebP, lazy)
Wrap the Cover in a link with an icon overlay only when the Artwork has a detail page (images.length > 1 || video)
Display title, year, medium beneath each

tasks.title: Build Ceramics Gallery page
tasks.description: Ceramics from the collection in a masonry-style grid.
steps: Create src/pages/ceramics.astro using BaseLayout
Query the ceramics collection with getCollection()
Render one Cover (images[0]) per Artwork in a masonry/variable-height grid with <Image> (AVIF+WebP, lazy)
Wrap the Cover in a link with an icon overlay when the Artwork has a detail page (images.length > 1 || video)
Display title, year, medium on hover or below each

tasks.title: Build Artwork detail pages
tasks.description: Dedicated static page per Artwork that has more to show. Data-driven, uniform across both collections.
steps: Create src/pages/paintings/[slug].astro and src/pages/ceramics/[slug].astro
getStaticPaths() emits a route only for entries where images.length > 1 || video is set
Render the full image set with <Picture> and the YouTube embed when present
Pass the Artwork's Cover as the BaseLayout ogImage so sharing the page previews the piece
Show title, year, medium; leave a reserved spot for the deferred per-Artwork buy button

tasks.title: Build Contact page
tasks.description: Photo on the left, form on the right, wired to Netlify Forms.
steps: Create src/pages/contact.astro using BaseLayout
Photo on the left side
Static HTML form on the right (data-netlify, hidden form-name, honeypot) with Name, Email, Subject, Message, all required
Set the form action to redirect to the custom thank-you page
Keep the form as server-rendered HTML, NOT inside a client island (Netlify scans built HTML)

tasks.title: Build thank-you page
tasks.description: Custom success route the contact form redirects to after submission.
steps: Create src/pages/thank-you.astro using BaseLayout
On-brand confirmation message with a link back home

tasks.title: Build custom 404 page
tasks.description: On-brand not-found page (Astro serves src/pages/404.astro for unmatched static routes).
steps: Create src/pages/404.astro using BaseLayout
Friendly message with links back to Home and the galleries

tasks.title: Per-Artwork buy button (e-commerce)
tasks.description: Embeddable Stripe/Shopify-style buy button as an island on Artwork detail pages. No standalone Shop page. Operational concerns (one-of-a-kind inventory, fragile shipping, tax) are the real challenge.

tasks.title: Self-serve admin uploads
tasks.description: Admin page to add Artworks: magic-link auth (allowlist checked server-side), a Netlify Function validates the JWT and commits Base64-encoded images to Git (Trees API), triggering a redeploy. Cover changes stay manual. Video stays external (URL only).
